Important message from University Health Service

Special Arrangement for the University Health Service (UHS)
from 29 January 2020 to 2 February 2020
 
Further to today’s announcement made to all staff from the Task Force on Infectious Diseases titled “Special Work Arrangements after Chinese New Year Holidays”, we would like to provide further details about the operation of the UHS during 29 January 2020 to 2 February 2020.

The UHS will maintain operation during this period.  However, in order to reduce the risk of the spread of the Novel Coronavirus Infection (2019-nCoV), some previously scheduled non-urgent appointments will be postponed.  Patients with non-urgent appointments during this period are also advised to call the respective UHS clinics to reschedule their appointments to a later date.  At the same time, the UHS will redeploy resources to dedicate fully to handle consultations and associated care related to the 2019-nCoV threat.  It will respond to requests and referrals from the campus residential community of students and visitors, especially those returning from affected areas. 

For 2019-nCoV matters,   please contact the UHS at 39172514

For appointment matters, please contact the following:
Medical appointments            2549 4686
Dental appointments             2859 2511,  2859 2512
Physiotherapy appointments  3917 2509

Thank you.



University Health Service
28 January 2020

Dear Colleagues, In light of the latest developments of the novel coronavirus, the University has decided to implement special work arrangements after the Chinese New Year holidays (starting from January 29). University offices will be closed to students, staff and the public until February 2, 2020. Heads of relevant departments will arrange for core staff to work on campus as needed to provide emergency and essential services. Other staff members shall work from home and are not required to return to the offices during this period. Heads of Departments will communicate with their staff members separately. During the period from January 29 to February 2, 2020, the Library, Learning Commons, University Health Service (UHS) clinics and sports facilities under the Centre for Sports and Exercise will be closed. On-campus catering will continue on a reduced basis, for which separate announcement will be made. The UHS will continue to support the University on matters related to the novel coronavirus. Students and staff with questions on novel coronavirus infection may contact the UHS at 3917-2514 during office hours. The University will continue to monitor and assess the situation, and keep the University community posted. Task Force on Infectious Diseases

有冇人識開聯署
我相信有九成人都極度唔鍾意zoom meeting
想開個聯署要求校方由week 3起將zoom meeting 全部轉為video and live learning，以及取消take attendance嘅做法
理由一:
zoom meeting令到學生無法投入於課堂，上課氣氛十分尷尬，令到老師教學節奏減慢
例子: 同學在開cam或mic後感到極不自在，老師問問題後經常dead air，而老師亦不習慣如此教學模式。
理由二:
zoom meeting沒有錄影，同學無法重溫上堂嘅內容。當有lag或斷線嘅情況，同學需要要求老師重複內容，或直接miss咗。
理由三:
同學家中並沒有合適嘅環境以及設備上堂。受新型冠狀病毒的影響，同學大部分時間都在家中上課，但部份同學家中過於嘈吵，或有工程進行中，都大大影響上課時嘅進度。
理由四：
現在社會環境惡劣，因同學們有機會外出購買生活用品，例如口罩，廁紙等，未必不能夠在特定準時上課，再者online learning嘅目的在於flexible，因此校方應取消Take attendance嘅做法。
取代方案:
一、將zoom meeting改為live learning or video learning，讓同學能夠重溫課堂內容。
二、將zoom meeting改為不開cam，不開mic嘅形式，並要求老師錄製課堂內容。
三、取消Take attendance嘅分數，例如將10%平分於其他計分項目，或直接以90%作為總分

以上係聯署嘅重點
有冇大佬英文夠好，有識開聯署嘅可以幫手開開。又或者翻譯做英文俾我哋做投訴信寄俾office又得。

Objection to Zoom Meeting and Taking Attendance For Online Learning- A Complaint Letter to the University of Hong Kong 致香港大學的投訴信 : 反對使用Zoom Meeting 以及課堂點名的做法
Dear HKU,

We are writing to complain about the use of Zoom Meeting for online learning and the section of taking attendance during online learning in Zoom Meeting. We now request the University of Hong Kong for turning all zoom meeting lesson into video and live learning and cancel out the taking attendance section.

Reason One:
When students are having live lesson on Zoom Meeting and turn on their mic and camera, they feel so embarrassed and uncomfortable. It makes them unable to enjoy and focus on the lesson. Also, it may slow down the teaching efficiency of professors and tutors. For example, there are always some "dead air" after the questions asked by teachers.

Reason Two:
There are no recordings and videos after the lesson. When the internet disconnects, students may easily miss out some important information from teachers. Students also cannot revise the content of the lectures and tutorial.

Reason Three:
Because of the impacts of Novel coronavirus, students usually have the meeting at home. However, most of them usually do not have a suitable learning environment and equipment. For example, some may be under a noisy environment.

Reason Four:
The aim of online learning is to allow students to follow up the missing lectures and tutorials. Being flexible is extremely essential. However, under nowadays society's condition, students sometimes have to go outside to buy some necessities such as surgical masks and toilet papers. They may not able to follow the original time session having the lesson online. Therefore, the university should cancel out the attendance taking section.

Alternative 1:
To turn all zoom meeting sections into live learning or video learning, including Panopto. It allows students to revise the lectures and tutorials.

Alternative 2:
To make sure all zoom meeting do not require students to turn on their camera and microphones and require teachers to record the lesson.

Alternative 3:

To cancel out the section of taking attendance.

We hope that the University of Hong Kong will accept our suggestion and understand our concern.
Best Regards,
HKU Students,Staff and Alumni members


Disclaimer: This open letter does not represent the stance of the University and other HKU students, alumni, and staff who have not signed this letter.

理由一:
zoom meeting令到學生無法投入於課堂，上課氣氛十分尷尬，令到老師教學節奏減慢
例子: 同學在開cam或mic後感到極不自在，老師問問題後經常dead air，而老師亦不習慣如此教學模式。
理由二:
zoom meeting沒有錄影，同學無法重溫上堂嘅內容。當有lag或斷線嘅情況，同學需要要求老師重複內容，或直接miss咗。
理由三:
同學家中並沒有合適嘅環境以及設備上堂。受新型冠狀病毒的影響，同學大部分時間都在家中上課，但部份同學家中過於嘈吵，或有工程進行中，都大大影響上課時嘅進度。
理由四：
現在社會環境惡劣，因同學們有機會外出購買生活用品，例如口罩，廁紙等，未必不能夠在特定準時上課，再者online learning嘅目的在於flexible，因此校方應取消Take attendance嘅做法。
取代方案:
一、將所有zoom meeting改為live learning or video learning(包括使用Panopto)，讓同學能夠重溫課堂內容。
二、將zoom meeting改為不開cam，不開mic嘅形式，並要求老師錄製課堂內容。
三、取消Take attendance嘅分數，例如將10%平分於其他計分項目，或直接以90%作為總分

請求校方務必採納同學們嘅意見，我們為求的只是提升同學的學習效率以及改善網上學習的質素。

一眾香港大學學生、教職員及畢業生

my ver:

Dear Prof. Holiday,

I totally understand that in view of the current situation, the university has no choice by to conduct lessons via ZOOM. However, ZOOM is not a suitable medium for teaching and learning.

ZOOM allows students and teachers to meet online and interact with each other. Yet, it has 2 major weaknesses that greatly hinder its effectiveness.

1. There is no recording available for students to review and revise the lessons on ZOOM.

2. ZOOM is often unstable as most teachers require students to open the face camera, causing delays or disruption of visual/ audio connection. As there is no recordings, students would often find it hard to catch up once he/ she is disconnected on ZOOM accidentally.

My suggestion would be that for all lectures and tutorial to conduct via Panopto instead of ZOOM, such that it could cater the needs of students to revise the lessons.

Thank you.

Regards,
<NAME>

zoom meeting令到學生無法投入於課堂，上課氣氛十分尷尬，令到老師教學節奏減慢
例子: 同學在開cam或mic後感到極不自在，老師問問題後經常dead air，而老師亦不習慣如此教學模式。
理由二:
zoom meeting沒有錄影，同學無法重溫上堂嘅內容。當有lag或斷線嘅情況，同學需要要求老師重複內容，或直接miss咗。
理由三:
同學家中並沒有合適嘅環境以及設備上堂。受新型冠狀病毒的影響，同學大部分時間都在家中上課，但部份同學家中過於嘈吵，或有工程進行中，都大大影響上課時嘅進度。
理由四：
現在社會環境惡劣，因同學們有機會外出購買生活用品，例如口罩，廁紙等，未必不能夠在特定準時上課，再者online learning嘅目的在於flexible，因此校方應取消Take attendance嘅做法。
取代方案:
一、將所有zoom meeting改為live learning or video learning(包括使用Panopto)，讓同學能夠重溫課堂內容。
二、將zoom meeting改為不開cam，不開mic嘅形式，並要求老師錄製課堂內容。
三、取消Take attendance嘅分數，例如將10%平分於其他計分項目，或直接以90%作為總分

請求校方務必採納同學們嘅意見，我們為求的只是提升同學的學習效率以及改善網上學習的質素。

一眾香港大學學生、教職員及畢業生

*此聲明不代表香港大學校方及沒有參與聯署的香港大學學生、校友及職員之立場。

Add/drop

First, a reminder. We extended the add/drop period to 4:00pm on Friday, February 21. So the window remains open only for a couple more days.

Late drop

We understand that the current online learning experience, while good for some students, is not positive for everyone. We’re therefore offering UG and TPG students the opportunity to late drop any of their Semester 2 courses at any point until 11:59pm on Thursday, April 30. Please note that at present we’re not planning to offer students a pass/fail option for letter-grade courses. Provided we’re able to return to campus teaching on March 30, as scheduled, we’ll stick with letter grading for this semester.

Feedback

We’re eager to learn about your experience with online learning to improve our courses. We’ve therefore created a webpage for anonymous feedback: http://hku.to/onlinelearningfeedback. If you’d like to receive a response from us, you can send an email to hcm00101@hku.hk.

Engage!

Online learning is very different from campus teaching, but it does still provide students with multiple opportunities to engage with their courses. Please do participate as fully as possible in interactive classes. As you know, there are many ways to do that on platforms such as Zoom. Please do also keep in touch with your course teachers and academic advisers through any of the platforms they use. Teachers and advisers are happy to connect virtually with students.



Best wishes, Ian
